confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_b506be04-f0a0-46ff-93e4-af8152e57ffb read back as promoted

evidence: narada task work-next --agent narada-cpy.builder returned No work available.
Immediately afterward, narada task list --limit 10 showed task 2 opened: Create CPY diagnostics command surface and related-systems registry.
proposal: When work-next suppresses an open task because of claimed/in_review/concurrency/affinity policy, return a blocked_or_hidden_work section naming the task and the exact reason.
Add a suggested next command, such as wait for review, ask architect to review tasks 1 and 3, or override concurrency with explicit command if allowed.
payload.recommendation: Improve task work-next explainability before relying on it as the natural role-loop entrypoint.
payload.summary: While processing CPY tasks, task work-next for narada-cpy.builder returned No work available, but task list showed task 2 still opened. The likely reason is a concurrency/review posture: the same builder has tasks 1 and 3 in_review. The CLI should explain that reason instead of making an open task look invisible.
payload.title: task work-next should explain why open tasks are unavailable
